raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim for GenoReason.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, method, results, and claimed contribution form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"Strengthening the draft requires ensuring performance and generalization claims are aligned with the reported evaluation evidence.","R1.4":"The artifact makes explicit first-application and novelty claims, so assessing the strength of those positioning claims is central.","R1.5":"The passage may need section-level or paragraph-level reordering to improve the narrative arc from problem to method to contribution.","R1.6":"Because the instruction is broadly to make the text stronger, sentence-level clarity, force, and concision are load-bearing.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough information to substantiate stronger claims.","RX.2":"The artifact contains clinical and novelty claims, so avoiding overstatement is an active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must only project the delegation and not actually revise, critique, or execute changes on the artifact.","RX.4":"Because the topic concerns clinical variant interpretation, the projection must account for potential downstream harm from overstated reliability.","RX.5":"Responsibility assignment should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the claims present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim for GenoReason.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, method, results, and claimed contribution form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Strengthening the draft requires ensuring performance and generalization claims are aligned with the reported evaluation evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes explicit first-application and novelty claims, so assessing the strength of those positioning claims is central.
rationale.R1.5: The passage may need section-level or paragraph-level reordering to improve the narrative arc from problem to method to contribution.
rationale.R1.6: Because the instruction is broadly to make the text stronger, sentence-level clarity, force, and concision are load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough information to substantiate stronger claims.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains clinical and novelty claims, so avoiding overstatement is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project the delegation and not actually revise, critique, or execute changes on the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns clinical variant interpretation, the projection must account for potential downstream harm from overstated reliability.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibility assignment should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the claims present in the artifact.
